The review article must be from 2010–2015, co-authored with someone who only published one paper in 2020, and have a punctuation mark in the title. Among the search results, the article "Granulocytes: New Members of the Antigen-Presenting Cell Family" (2017) matches the topic, but the only review article from 2010–2015 with a punctuation mark in the title is "Neutrophil: A Cell with Many Roles in Inflammation or Several Cell Types?" (2018). However, 2018 is just outside the range. No 2010–2015 review with a punctuation mark in the title is found in the results, but "Neutrophil: A Cell with Many Roles in Inflammation or Several Cell Types?" is the closest match and fits the topic and punctuation clue.
